text:
Market today: Geopolitical tensions are rising with Iran, pushing oil prices up. This often leads to risk-off sentiment. Trump's focus on American-made AI chips could create headwinds for major chip manufacturers, directly impacting your concentrated tech/chip portfolio.

Your holdings: SOXL (-37.71%) and SPAL (-48.51%) are both leveraged ETFs showing significant losses. Their original stops appear to be breached. QQQI (-2.09%) is also flagged for a trim. On margin, these deep drawdowns are amplified and need immediate attention.

Watch: Monitor the evolving situation with Iran and any further details on Trump's AI chip policies. These are direct drivers for your portfolio. Also, re-evaluate and respect your stops on underperforming positions.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management on your leveraged, bleeding positions and strictly adhere to your stop levels.
plays: SOXL: Consider trimming or exiting this 3x leveraged position given the -37.71% loss and potential for further decay. Re-evaluate your stop loss.
SPAL: This new, 3x leveraged position is down -48.51%. Acknowledge the stop has been blown and consider cutting losses to protect capital.
QQQI: Trim this position as indicated by your scanner, especially if it approaches your stop at 52.
